Purpose: Core data object for a Cyboquatic node, recording its state and impact.
Key Fields / Coordinates: node_id, lat, lon, medium, q_m3s, depth_m, vel_ms, sed_conc_mgL, gw_head_up_m, edna_reads_total, biodiv_index, r_pfbs, r_ecoli, r_sat, r_do, r_sed_excess, r_sed_deficit, r_recharge, r_biodiversity, r_trap, r_hydraulic, r_fragment_aquatic, r_fragment_terrestrial, vt, k_factor, e_factor, r_factor, lane, evidence_hex
Governing Principle: Single source of truth for governance, with all fields contributing to the Lyapunov residual.

Purpose: Configuration file defining corridor bands for all risk coordinates.
Key Fields / Coordinates: corridorvarid, regionid, guild, coordinate, band_type (safe/gold/hard), value_min, value_max, weight
Governing Principle: Provides the context-specific parameters that translate raw data into risk scores.

Purpose: Engine for spatial optimization, proposing configurations based on connectivity metrics.
Key Fields / Coordinates: node_id, lat, lon, adjacent_land_use, distance_to_next_refuge, habitat_reach_length, corridor_ID, r_fragment_aquatic, r_fragment_terrestrial
Governing Principle: Treats node placement as a network design problem under KER constraints, maximizing connectivity benefits.

Purpose: Non-actuating diagnostic tool output for validating hydrological models.
Key Fields / Coordinates: scenario_id, event_duration, peak_discharge, delta_rhydraulic, delta_rsed_excess, delta_rsed_deficit, final_vt
Governing Principle: Empirically calibrates short-term vs. long-term corridors by simulating extreme events.

Purpose: Non-actuating diagnostic tool output for validating ecological trap risk assessments.
Key Fields / Coordinates: node_id, guild, attraction_index, survival_rate, r_trap_value, trap_risk_level (low/medium/high)
Governing Principle: Audits designs against trap-risk corridors, informing the NodePlacementShard.
